steps.title: Take the square root of both sides
steps.content:
From $(x - 35)^2 = 1$, take the square root of both sides to eliminate the squared expression:
$$x - 35 = \pm 1$$

steps.title: Solve both equations and sum the solutions
steps.content:
From $x - 35 = 1$, we get $x = 36$.
From $x - 35 = -1$, we get $x = 34$.
The sum is $36 + 34 = 70$.
